title: Redundant Connection (Union-Find)
problem_statement: Return the edge that can be removed from a graph so the remaining edges form a tree.

examples.explanation: Removing edge [2,3] eliminates the only cycle.

intuition: Use union-find and return the first edge that connects nodes already in the same component.

approaches.name: Union-Find cycle detection
approaches.description: For each edge, if both endpoints share a root, that edge is redundant and forms a cycle.
approaches.why_not: DFS cycle detection is possible but union-find directly identifies the redundant edge during insertion.

interview_qa.q: Why is the first cycle-causing edge the answer?
interview_qa.a: Because the graph starts as a tree and only one extra edge creates exactly one cycle.

interview_qa.q: What does union-find detect every time?
interview_qa.a: Whether two nodes are already connected through previous edges.
